error.error_equation: (a+b+c)^2=a^2+b^2+c^2+a b+a c+b a+b c+c a+c b \\ & \sqrt{138+131 \times 6}=2 \sqrt{231}
error.teacher_advice_ch: 138為三個數的平方和，131為取兩數乘積之和，直接相減開更號無法得出三數的平均，這題可以使用的公式為 (a+b+c)^2 = a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + 2ab +2ac + abc ，因此可以將138+2*131的值開根號得出答案
error.teacher_advice_en: 138 is the sum of the squares of the three numbers, and 131 is the sum of the products of the numbers taken two at a time. Subtracting the two values directly and taking the square root will not give the average of the three numbers. The formula that can be used for this problem is (a+b+c)^2 = a^2 + b^2 + c^2 + 2ab +2ac + abc. Therefore, the square root of 138+2*131 will give the answer.
